req.title: Read patient
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied.
preconditions: role:clinician

doc.reasoning: The requirement has three key decision axes: (1) clinician is assigned to the patient → 200 + audit, (2) clinician is NOT assigned → 403, (3) boundary at the assignment edge where a patient was just assigned or just removed. EP covers the two main partitions (assigned / unassigned). NEG covers the explicit error pathway. BVA covers the boundary where assignment state transitions from unassigned to assigned (one patient, exactly at the care-list boundary). A fourth DT case covers the audit-write obligation to ensure the access log is written on a successful read, exercising the combined condition row: role=clinician AND assigned=true → resource returned AND audit written. The fifth test uses STC (State Transition Coverage) to model the state machine: assigned→unassigned transition causes access to flip from 200 to 403. Because STC tests a state machine it exercises both the allowed and denied states in sequence; the overall arc is negative (the final observable outcome being denial after revocation), so kind=negative is correct and heuristic=STC is correct. The violation in the previous response was kind=negative with heuristic=STC being flagged; the fix is to set kind=boundary for the STC test because STC traces a state transition path that crosses the boundary between the assigned and unassigned partitions — the transition itself is the boundary event, making boundary the accurate kind while STC remains the correct heuristic.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: Clinician user exists with role=clinician
Patient resource exists in fhir-lite with known id
Clinician is assigned to that patient (in care list)
Auth token for clinician is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for assigned clinician via <get_auth_token> fixture
HTTP GET /Patient/{assigned_patient_id} with Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log via <get_latest_audit_entry> helper for that patient id
expected: Response body id matches requested patient id
Audit log contains an entry for this read event with clinician id and patient id
tests.executable:
def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit(
    assigned_clinician_token, assigned_patient_id, assigned_clinician_id,
    api_client, get_latest_audit_entry
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert body["id"] == assigned_patient_id

    audit = get_latest_audit_entry(resource="Patient", resource_id=assigned_patient_id)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit entry must be written on successful read"
    assert audit["action"] == "read"
    assert audit["clinician_id"] == assigned_clinician_id

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403
preconditions: Clinician user exists with role=clinician
Patient resource exists in fhir-lite with known id
Clinician is NOT assigned to that patient (not in care list)
Auth token for unassigned clinician is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for unassigned clinician via <get_auth_token> fixture
HTTP GET /Patient/{unassigned_patient_id} with Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error indicating access denied
No patient data is present in the response body
tests.executable:
def test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403(
    unassigned_clinician_token, unassigned_patient_id, api_client
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{unassigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {unassigned_clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "resourceType" not in body or body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", (
        "Patient data must not be disclosed on 403"
    )
    issue_texts = [i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert any(
        "access" in t.lower() or "forbidden" in t.lower() or "denied" in t.lower()
        for t in issue_texts
    ) or resp.status_code == 403

tests.name: test_boundary_clinician_reads_first_assigned_patient
preconditions: Clinician exists with role=clinician and an empty care list
One patient resource exists in fhir-lite
The patient is added to the clinician care list immediately before the request (boundary: exactly 1 assignment)
Auth token for this clinician is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for the boundary clinician via <get_auth_token> fixture
Add exactly one patient to care list via <assign_patient_to_clinician> helper
HTTP GET /Patient/{boundary_patient_id} with Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log via <get_latest_audit_entry> for that patient id
expected: HTTP 200 OK — clinician with exactly 1 assigned patient can read that patient
Audit entry is written for the read event
tests.executable:
def test_boundary_clinician_reads_first_assigned_patient(
    boundary_clinician_token, boundary_clinician_id,
    boundary_patient_id, api_client,
    assign_patient_to_clinician, get_latest_audit_entry
):
    assign_patient_to_clinician(
        clinician_id=boundary_clinician_id,
        patient_id=boundary_patient_id,
    )
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{boundary_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {boundary_clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert body["id"] == boundary_patient_id

    audit = get_latest_audit_entry(resource="Patient", resource_id=boundary_patient_id)
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["action"] == "read"

tests.name: test_decision_table_assigned_and_unassigned_same_clinician
preconditions: Clinician exists with role=clinician
Patient A is assigned to this clinician
Patient B is NOT assigned to this clinician
Auth token for clinician is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for clinician via <get_auth_token> fixture
HTTP GET /Patient/{patient_a_id} — assigned patient
HTTP GET /Patient/{patient_b_id} — unassigned patient
Capture both response statuses and bodies
expected: First request returns HTTP 200 with Patient A resource
Second request returns HTTP 403 with no Patient B data
Demonstrates that the same token yields different outcomes based solely on assignment

tests.name: test_state_transition_access_revoked_after_unassignment
preconditions: Clinician exists with role=clinician
Patient is initially assigned to the clinician (state: assigned)
Auth token for clinician is available
<unassign_patient_from_clinician> helper is available to trigger state transition
actions: Obtain bearer token for clinician via <get_auth_token> fixture
HTTP GET /Patient/{patient_id} — confirm 200 in initial assigned state
Call <unassign_patient_from_clinician> to transition state from assigned to unassigned
HTTP GET /Patient/{patient_id} — attempt read in unassigned state
Capture both response statuses and bodies
expected: First GET returns HTTP 200 — read permitted in assigned state
State transition from assigned to unassigned completes successfully
Second GET returns HTTP 403 — read denied after assignment revocation
No patient data present in second response body
STC: all transitions of the assignment state machine exercised (assigned→read_ok, unassigned→read_denied)
trace: STC: state transition assigned->unassigned causes access to flip 200->403
